compiled_state.title: [GREEN] Implement summary subcommand in ticket CLI dispatcher with cycle detection
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Implement the `summary` subcommand in the ticket CLI dispatcher with cycle detection and dangling link handling. Requires a new ticket-summary.sh script since issue-summary.sh lacks these behaviors.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-summary.sh (new — one-line summary with cycle detection and dangling link handling)
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (modify — add summary case + update _usage())
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh (modify — add ticket_summary() function)

## Acceptance Criteria

### ticket-summary.sh:
- [ ] Accepts: `<id> [<id2> ...]` (one or more ticket IDs)
- [ ] Output format: `<id> [<status>] <title> (ready)` or `<id> [<status>] <title> (blocked by: <dep-ids>)` — matches issue-summary.sh format exactly
- [ ] Uses TICKET_CMD env var to call `ticket show` (injectable for testing)
- [ ] Maintains a VISITED set during dep traversal — if a dep ID is already visited, outputs `<id> [cycle detected]` and does NOT recurse further (prevents infinite loops)
- [ ] When `ticket show <dep-id>` returns empty or fails (dangling link target), outputs `<dep-id>[dangling]` in the blocked-by list instead of crashing
- [ ] Executable bit set: `chmod +x plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-summary.sh`
- [ ] issue-summary.sh is NOT modified (scope boundary)

### Dispatcher routing:
- [ ] `ticket_summary()` added to ticket-lib-api.sh: `bash "$_TICKETLIB_DIR/ticket-summary.sh" "$@"; return $?`
- [ ] `summary)` case added to dispatcher after `purge-bridge)` case: `_ensure_initialized && source ticket-lib-api.sh && _ticketlib_dispatch ticket_summary "$@" && exit $?`
- [ ] _usage() string updated: "summary One-line ticket summary with blocking status (<id> [<id2>...])"
- [ ] SOURCEABILITY CONTRACT preserved; _TICKETLIB_DIR used for path
- [ ] Task b40c-dc7e RED tests turn GREEN after implementation

## Notes
The cycle detection VISITED set should track ticket IDs that have been processed during the current invocation. Simple implementation: declare a space-separated string or associative array of visited IDs. Before fetching deps for an ID, check if it's already in VISITED; if so, output the cycle warning and skip. The dangling link check is: if `ticket show <dep-id>` exits non-zero or returns empty JSON, treat as dangling.
